title: FIXFlux - Distributed Traces
description: Saved TraceQL queries for tracing single orders across the order-service → risk-service → matching-engine → trade-store pipeline

panels.title: Full Order Lifecycle (reached trade-store)
panels.description:
Orders that completed the full happy path and were persisted as a trade. Filtered to `trade-store.persist` spans — if this span exists, the order passed risk, was matched, and is in the database.

Click any row to open the span waterfall and see timing for each service hop.

TraceQL: `{name="trade-store.persist"}`

panels.title: Risk-Approved Orders (reached matching engine)
panels.description:
Orders that cleared the pre-trade risk check and entered the matching engine. If a `matching-engine.process` span exists, the risk-service approved the order.

TraceQL: `{name="matching-engine.process"}`

panels.title: All Risk Evaluations (approved + rejected)
panels.description:
All spans from the risk-service order evaluation. Span name is `risk-service.validated_orders` for every order regardless of outcome — risk-service consumes from `validated_orders` and decides internally whether to approve or reject.

To see only rejections, cross-reference with the Loki 'Rejected Orders Only' panel using the `order.id` span attribute.

TraceQL: `{name="risk-service.validated_orders"}`

panels.title: Slow Matching Engine Spans (> 50 ms)
panels.description:
Matching-engine spans that took longer than 50 ms — useful for spotting order book contention or GC pauses under load.

Adjust the threshold in the query editor to tighten or loosen the filter.

TraceQL: `{name="matching-engine.process" && duration > 50ms}`

panels.title: All Spans (any instrumented service)
panels.description:
Every span from every instrumented service — order-service, risk-service, matching-engine, and trade-store. Useful for a broad overview of recent activity or for finding spans when you do not know which service to look at first.

TraceQL: `{resource.service.name=~"order-service|risk-service|matching-engine|trade-store"}`

panels.title: Error Spans (any service)
panels.description:
Any span that finished with status=error. In normal operation this panel should be empty — if rows appear, click through to see the exception attached to the failing span.

TraceQL: `{status=error}`
